title: PQC Today — Quick Overview
whatToExpect: A 6-minute tour of every PQC Today section
Pointers into the right module for your role and industry
Where to find authoritative standards (NIST, CCCS, ASD, ENISA)
What to read next once you've picked your persona

steps.title: Welcome to PQC Today
steps.whyItMatters: Sets the destination — a vendor-neutral resource for the post-quantum cryptography migration.
steps.expectedOutput: You can describe PQC Today in one sentence.
steps.narration: Welcome to PQC Today — a free, vendor-neutral resource for the post-quantum cryptography migration. We will tour every page in about six minutes.

cues.text: Welcome to PQC Today — vendor-neutral resource for the post-quantum migration.
We will tour every page in about six minutes.

steps.title: Persona picker — pick your role
steps.whyItMatters: The persona switcher tailors the entire app — modules, hints, journey steps, recommended actions — to your role, proficiency, industry, and region.
tasks: Pick a role that fits you
steps.expectedOutput: You know how to set your persona context.
steps.narration: The persona switcher in the top-right tailors every page to your role — executive, developer, architect, ops, researcher, or curious — plus your proficiency, industry, and region.

cues.text: Persona switcher — tailor every page to your role, proficiency, industry, and region.
Six roles: executive, developer, architect, ops, researcher, curious.

steps.title: Learn — 40+ modules
steps.whyItMatters: Learn is the index of every module on PQC Today. Each module has Learn / Visual / Workshop / Exercises / References / Tools tabs.
tasks: Note the role-tailored block at the top
steps.expectedOutput: Mental model of how the Learn dashboard is organised.
steps.narration: Learn is the index of every module on PQC Today. Forty modules across foundations, role guides, executive deep dives, industries, and protocols. Pick your persona to surface the right modules first.

cues.text: Learn — 40+ modules indexed across foundations, roles, industries, and protocols.
Role-tailored modules surface based on your active persona.
Every module has six tabs covering Learn, Visual, Workshop, Exercises, References, and Tools.
Quiz module validates your understanding at interview / boardroom speed.

steps.title: Walk PQC 101 — the foundation module
steps.whyItMatters: PQC 101 is the foundation module everyone starts with — Q-Day, Harvest-Now Decrypt-Later, the new NIST standards (FIPS 203 ML-KEM and FIPS 204 ML-DSA), the migration timeline, and what a ready org looks like.
tasks: Glance at the Visual + Workshop tabs
steps.expectedOutput: You can describe Q-Day, HNDL, and what FIPS 203/204 are in 60 seconds.
steps.narration: PQC 101 is the foundation module everyone starts with. Five sections: the quantum threat, the new algorithms, the migration timeline, the readiness picture, and the next steps. Six tabs surface the same content as text (Learn), diagrams (Visual), interactive demos (Workshop), short quizzes (Exercises), source documents (References), and vendor tooling (Tools & Products).

cues.text: PQC 101 — PQC 101 is the foundation module everyone starts with — Q-Day, Harvest-Now Decrypt-Later, the new NIST standards (FIPS 203 ML-KEM and FIPS 204 ML-DSA), the migration timeline, and what a ready org looks like.
Section 1/5: The Quantum Threat to Cryptography
Section 2/5: NIST-Selected PQC Algorithms
Section 3/5: Global Migration Timeline
Section 4/5: Organizational Readiness
Section 5/5: Your Next Steps
Hands-on 1/4: Algorithm Families
Hands-on 2/4: Algorithm Comparison
Hands-on 3/4: Key Generation
Hands-on 4/4: Signature Demo
Visual tab — diagrams and animations for this topic.
Exercises — short knowledge checks to confirm you got it.
References — primary sources every claim is cited from.
Tools & Products — vendor tooling and PQC-ready software.

steps.title: Timeline — every country's PQC runway
steps.whyItMatters: Timeline is the runway. Each caption below names a country heading on the page so the page auto-scrolls through major jurisdictions in real time.
tasks: Watch the page scroll through major countries
steps.expectedOutput: You see milestones for every major PQC jurisdiction.
steps.narration: Timeline is your migration runway. Every country block is its own section — the next 70 seconds walks through the major jurisdictions: United States, European Union, Canada, Australia, Japan, United Kingdom, G7.

cues.text: United States
European Union
Canada
Australia
Japan
United Kingdom
G7

steps.title: Algorithms — NIST PQC standards
steps.whyItMatters: Two views: Transition Guide maps classical→PQC; Detailed Comparison shows every algorithm's key sizes, performance, FIPS status.
tasks: Watch the page switch tabs + scroll into each section
steps.expectedOutput: You can name the FIPS number for each new standard.
steps.narration: Algorithms has two views — Transition Guide for classical→PQC mappings, and Detailed Comparison for per-algorithm specs. ML-KEM (FIPS 203), ML-DSA (FIPS 204), SLH-DSA (FIPS 205).

cues.text: Algorithms — NIST PQC standards + classical comparison.
Transition Guide — what to swap and when.
Detailed Comparison — key sizes, performance, FIPS status.

steps.title: Threats — industry-specific catalogue
steps.whyItMatters: Threats are sorted by industry. Each row links to evidence, affected protocols, and severity.
steps.expectedOutput: You can name one HIGH-severity threat affecting your stack.
steps.narration: Threats catalogues every public quantum-related threat by industry. Each row shows severity, affected protocols, and evidence links. Bookmark the ones that affect your stack.

cues.text: Threats — industry-specific catalogue with severity + evidence.
FIN-001 — example: a finance HIGH-severity threat.
Each row links to evidence + affected protocols. Bookmark for planning.

steps.title: Library — standards, RFCs, specs
steps.whyItMatters: Library is the citable source set. Search any standard ID; bookmark for inline reference in your reports.
steps.expectedOutput: You can find any cited standard in <30 seconds.
steps.narration: Library is the source set behind every claim on PQC Today. NIST FIPS / IR / SP, IETF RFCs, ISO / ETSI / ENISA / CCCS / ASD documents — all searchable, all linkable.

cues.text: Library — every standard, RFC, and spec cited on PQC Today.
NIST publishes FIPS, I R, and S P. IETF publishes R F Cs. Then ISO, ETSI, ENISA, CCCS, and ASD.
Bookmark for inline reference in your reports.

steps.title: Leaders — who is publicly leading PQC
steps.whyItMatters: Leaders surfaces the named PQC champions across government, finance, healthcare, telecom — the citations your board will recognise.
tasks: Bookmark a citation for your next memo
steps.expectedOutput: You can name 1-2 in-country PQC leaders.
steps.narration: Leaders is the named-individuals catalogue. Government PQC officials, financial-sector champions, telecom and healthcare leaders. Filter by your country and you have board-grade citations.

cues.text: Leaders — named PQC champions across government and industry.
Filter by your country to surface in-jurisdiction citations.

steps.title: Migrate — vendor + product catalogue
steps.whyItMatters: Migrate is the catalogue of vendors and products with PQC roadmaps + certifications. The list you cite when filing tickets.
tasks: Filter by a vendor in your stack
steps.expectedOutput: You can identify which vendors in your stack have a PQC roadmap.
steps.narration: Migrate is the vendor + product catalogue. Each row links to public PQC roadmaps and to the certifications products carry — FIPS 140-3, ACVP, Common Criteria.

cues.text: Migrate — vendor + product catalogue with PQC roadmaps.
Filter by a vendor in your stack.
Open a product to see its FIPS / ACVP / CC certification status.

steps.title: Compliance — frameworks + binding deadlines
steps.whyItMatters: Compliance maps every published framework to deadlines, required algorithms, and source documents.
steps.expectedOutput: You can quote the framework + deadline + required algorithm for any review.
steps.narration: Compliance maps every published PQC framework to its binding deadline and the required algorithm set. NIST CNSA 2.0, CCCS ITSM.40.001, ASD ISM-1917, ENISA — all in one filter.

cues.text: PQC Compliance Deadlines
Standardization Bodies

steps.title: Assess — your personalised risk profile
steps.whyItMatters: Assess is the wizard that produces a personalised risk profile in 6-13 questions.
steps.expectedOutput: You see the assessment wizard mode picker.
steps.narration: Assess is the wizard. Quick mode is six questions and two minutes; Comprehensive is thirteen and five minutes. The output feeds your personalised report and seeds the Command Center artifacts.

cues.text: Assess — wizard producing a personalised risk profile.
Quick mode = 6 questions, 2 min. Comprehensive = 13 questions, 5 min.
Output feeds the Report and seeds Command Center artifacts.

steps.title: Report — board-ready output
steps.whyItMatters: Report is the board pack: shareable URL, hideable sections, and exportable PDF / DOCX.
steps.expectedOutput: You see your assessment-driven report or a placeholder if not run yet.
steps.narration: Report is your board pack. After running Assess, this page renders a personalised report with shareable URL, hideable sections, and PDF / DOCX export.

cues.text: Report — board pack with share URL and PDF / DOCX export.
Run Assess first to populate this page with your personalised content.

steps.title: Command Center — CSWP-39 strategic plan
steps.whyItMatters: Command Center organises every artifact (RACI, policy draft, KPI dashboard, board deck) by NIST CSWP-39 zone.
steps.expectedOutput: You can name the 6 CSWP-39 zones.
steps.narration: Command Center is your PQC artifact workspace. Six zones from NIST CSWP-39: Governance, Assets, Management Tools, Risk Management, Mitigation, Migration. Each zone has artifact builders (RACI, policy draft, KPI dashboard, board deck) you generate from your assessment data.

cues.text: Command Center — CSWP-39 strategic plan + artifact builders.
Six zones: Governance · Assets · Management Tools · Risk · Mitigation · Migration.
Each zone holds artifact builders — RACI, policy draft, KPI dashboard, board deck.

steps.title: Playground — interactive PQC + crypto demos
steps.whyItMatters: Playground runs real cryptographic operations in your browser — keygen, signing, KEM exchange — using OpenSSL WASM and liboqs.
tasks: Open one of the interactive workshops
steps.expectedOutput: You see the interactive crypto playground.
steps.narration: Playground is interactive crypto. Keygen, signing, KEM exchange — all running in your browser via OpenSSL WASM and liboqs. No server, no network round-trip, your keys stay local.

cues.text: Playground — interactive crypto running in your browser.
OpenSSL WASM + liboqs — no server, no network, keys stay local.

steps.title: Patents — PQC patent landscape
steps.whyItMatters: Patents catalogues the PQC IP landscape — algorithm patents, implementation patents, and assignee mapping.
steps.expectedOutput: You know where the PQC patent risk concentrations sit.
steps.narration: Patents catalogues the PQC IP landscape. Algorithm-level patents (Kyber, Dilithium predecessors), implementation patents, side-channel countermeasures. Useful when you negotiate licensing or evaluate vendor lock-in.

cues.text: Patents — PQC IP landscape: algorithms, implementations, side-channels.
Filter by assignee to spot concentration risk.

steps.title: Changelog — what is new on PQC Today
steps.whyItMatters: Changelog is how the app stays current — new modules, refreshed data, new vendors, updated standards.
steps.expectedOutput: You know where to check for app updates.
steps.narration: Changelog tracks every release. New modules, refreshed CSV data sets, new vendors in the migrate catalogue, updated standards in the library.

cues.text: Changelog — module + data + vendor + standard updates.

steps.title: About — trust + transparency
steps.whyItMatters: About is the trust page. Each caption literally names a section heading so the page auto-scrolls through the page in real time.
tasks: Watch the page scroll through About sections
steps.expectedOutput: You can describe the project trust model.
steps.narration: About is the trust page. The next minute walks through the page top-to-bottom: project intro, founding principles, who it is for, data privacy, security audit, open source license, and community.

cues.text: About PQC Today
Our founding principles
Who this is for
The timeline is not optional
Data Privacy
Security Audit
Open Source License
Community

steps.title: Quiz — test what you learned
steps.whyItMatters: A 5-to-45-minute quiz with persona-tuned topics gives you a quick measure of what stuck.
tasks: Note the duration slider and difficulty filter
See how persona-tuned topics are pre-selected
Run the quiz yourself after the workshop
steps.expectedOutput: A working knowledge of the quiz interface; you'll come back to actually take it.
steps.narration: The quiz is the quickest way to check what you've absorbed. Pick how much time you have, pick the topics that matter, and get a scored breakdown by category and difficulty.

cues.text: Test what you learned. Pick a duration, pick topics, go.
Duration slider. Five to forty-five minutes. Quiz scales the question count to match.
Difficulty filter. Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced. Multi-select.
Topic grid. Categories pre-filtered by your persona context.
Selected topics show a checkmark. The Start button activates once you pick at least one.
Start the quiz when you're ready. We'll skip running it inside the workshop.
Results show your score, category breakdown, difficulty breakdown, and let you retake or change topics.

steps.title: Pick a persona for the tailored workshop
steps.whyItMatters: The generic tour shows what is available. A persona-specific workshop tells you what to do — pick yours and re-open the Workshop tab.
steps.expectedOutput: You know how to switch from generic overview to a persona-specific workshop.
steps.narration: You have seen the surfaces. Pick a persona — Executive, Developer, Architect, Ops, Researcher, or Curious — and the Workshop tab will surface a flow tailored to your role.

cues.text: Pick a persona for a tailored workshop. Welcome to PQC Today.
